title: Clippy: 4 missing const fn opportunities
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

**Dimension:** Performance & Complexity
**Lint:** clippy::missing_const_for_fn

## Violation
Functions whose body is a trivially-const expression (only const-eligible operations, no trait calls beyond Copy, no heap allocation) should be marked `const fn` so callers can use them in const contexts (array sizes, static initializers, match arms).

## Locations
- src/tools/file/mod.rs: 36
- src/tui/messages.rs: 46, 52
- src/tui/mod.rs: 1052

## Mandated Refactor
Add `const` to the fn signature:
```rust
// Before
pub fn default_limit() -> usize { 100 }
// After
pub const fn default_limit() -> usize { 100 }
```
Then verify no callsite regressed — const fns are strictly more permissive, so this is almost always safe.
